title: n8n worker pod crashing with SIGTERM
symptom: The n8n worker pod keeps crashing and logs show 'Received SIGTERM. Shutting down...'. This prevents the worker from processing tasks.
applies_when: Using n8n in a self-hosted Kubernetes setup
Worker pod is configured with queue execution mode
not_this_if: Worker pod is running without crashing
root_cause: The worker pod is not able to respond to readiness probes, causing Kubernetes to send a SIGTERM signal to terminate the pod. This can happen if the health check is not properly configured, leading to the pod being marked as unhealthy.
fix_steps: Add the environment variable `QUEUE_HEALTH_CHECK_ACTIVE: true` to the worker pod configuration
Redeploy the worker pod to apply the changes
Monitor the logs to ensure the worker starts without crashing
agent_instruction: Set `QUEUE_HEALTH_CHECK_ACTIVE` to true in the worker pod configuration and redeploy.
